commit.message:
Redesign app UI with modern Material 3

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add dashboard UI with real-time active song syncing via useSyncedState (#12)

Adds a TV-friendly dashboard page (`/:token/dashboard`) for controlling
which song is currently playing, with real-time sync to all connected
vote pages via rwsdk's `useSyncedState`.

### Infrastructure
- Added `SyncedStateServer` Durable Object binding and v2 migration in
`wrangler.jsonc`
- Registered `syncedStateRoutes` and exported `SyncedStateServer` from
`worker.tsx`

### Dashboard (`/:token/dashboard`)
- Server component loads songs; client component (`DashboardControls`)
renders play/pause buttons per song
- State scoped to game ID via third parameter: `useSyncedState<string |
null>(null, "activeSong", gameId)`
- "Now playing" banner always reserves vertical space — shows dimmed "No
song currently playing" placeholder when idle, preventing layout shifts
- Accessible `aria-label`/`aria-pressed` on all buttons

### Vote page active song highlight
- `useActiveSong(gameId)` custom hook subscribes to the synced state key
- `VoteSongList` client component uses the hook and conditionally
applies Tailwind CSS `before:` pseudo-element classes
(`before:bg-linear-to-br before:from-indigo-500/35
before:via-purple-500/25 before:to-indigo-500/15`) for a translucent
indigo→purple gradient ring on the active song
- Custom pulsing animation defined in `styles.css` as a CSS custom
property (`--animate-active-song-pulse`), applied via
`animate-(--animate-active-song-pulse)`
- `aria-live="polite"` region announces the currently playing song for
assistive tech

### Screenshots

**Dashboard (no song playing)**
![Dashboard no
song](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/79f2d7c4-9f2a-4d38-9d00-d2c7a3e3c6ea)

**Vote page (active song highlighted)**
![Vote active
song](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/975b3e0b-d1d5-460f-8fb9-4aefda37219b)

### E2E tests (3 new, 19 total passing)
- Dashboard rendering and song selection/deselection
- Cross-page real-time sync: two browser pages open simultaneously,
clicking a song on the dashboard asserts that
`[data-testid="now-playing"]` updates on the vote page without refresh

```tsx
// Dashboard sets the active song scoped to this game
const [activeSong, setActiveSong] = useSyncedState<string | null>(null, "activeSong", gameId);

// Vote page subscribes via custom hook — updates arrive via WebSocket
const activeSong = useActiveSong(gameId);
```

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Show error dialog when shell not found on terminal start (#114)

Replace .unwrap() on PTY spawn with proper error handling. When the
configured shell program cannot be found or fails to start, an error
dialog is shown to the user instead of panicking.

- Add ShellErrorDialog component following existing dialog patterns
- Handle spawn errors at all call sites: new tab, split pane, and
workspace restore

commit.message:
fix: P95 domain adversarial patient_idx indexing bug

batch.patient_idx is per-graph [B] from PyG batching (scalar attrs are
stacked), NOT per-node [N]. Code was indexing with batch.ptr[gi] (a node
offset) which exceeds tensor size for any graph with >1 node.

Fix: use batch.patient_idx directly + store as torch.tensor for explicitness.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Agent: Resource Leak Patrol (#8360)

* Agent: Resource Leak Patrol

* Fix temp directory leak in TempFileStorageBackend when dispose() races with in-flight mkdtemp()

The dispose() method captured tempRootPath synchronously but did not
await the pending tempRootPathPromise. If mkdtemp() was still in-flight,
dispose() would see tempRootPath as null, skip cleanup, and the newly
created temp directory would be orphaned on disk.

Fix:
- dispose() now awaits the in-flight mkdtemp promise via a new
  resolveInflightTempPath() helper to reliably capture the created path
- ensureRootPath() guards the .then() callback against post-dispose
  state mutation so tempRootPath is not set after disposal

Tests:
- Added test verifying dispose cleans up when mkdtemp is in-flight
- Added test verifying the temp directory is removed from disk

Integrate single-run analysis findings into interpretation docs

- Add README contents table and mention 1run study variants
- Document hamilton_1run and prisoners_1run in instructions files
- Integrate 1run temporal dynamics findings into prisoners.md:
  - Phase boundary: low-cooperation values are genuine equilibria, not cycling
  - Pop_2 asymmetry: absorbing state, stochastic role assignment
  - Pop_3 tipping: single early jump, no cycling
  - C1P0 displacement: oscillatory coexistence, never completes
- Integrate 1run findings into hamilton.md:
  - Gradual transition confirmed genuine, not averaging artifact
  - Pop_2 roles lock in early; weaker correlation from low-b-c noise
  - Pop_3 jump: one-time tipping event
  - C1P1/C1P0 anti-phase oscillation at frequency-dependent equilibrium

fix: update duration from stream_details, clear stale duration on advance

Duration was not updating on track auto-advance because _advance_queue
sent the old track's duration_ms to Ynison, which echoed it back.

Three fixes:
- _advance_queue / _on_previous: set duration_ms=0 to prevent
  propagating stale duration from the previous track
- _update_metadata_from_stream: new method called from _stream_track
  that updates duration and elapsed_time from stream_details (the
  authoritative source) with force_update=True
- Import StreamDetails for the type annotation

commit.message:
fix: downgrade REQUEST_CHANGES to COMMENT on bot's own PRs (#51)

## Problem

PR #49 review failed with 422 — GitHub doesn't allow `REQUEST_CHANGES`
on your own PR. The bot created #49 via `/implement`, then tried to
review it with `REQUEST_CHANGES`.

## Fix

When reviewing a PR authored by the bot, downgrade `REQUEST_CHANGES` to
`COMMENT`. The feedback still gets posted, it just doesn't block.

---------

commit.message:
fix(copilot): register companion PTY in session store and hide from UI

The hidden companion terminal for agent tabs was not registered in the
session store during init/polling, causing TouchBar key presses to be
silently dropped. Additionally, hidden sessions were added to tabOrder,
making them visible in the side panel and session hub.

- Register companion PTY as hidden session during init and polling loops
  (matching handleNewSessionCreated behavior)
- Exclude hidden sessions from tabOrder in addSession() so they never
  appear in tabs, side panel, or session hub
- Prevent hidden sessions from becoming activeId automatically

commit.message:
fix: use MYSQL_PWD env var instead of .cnf file for MySQL passwords

MySQL .cnf files don't properly handle passwords with % characters
even when quoted, as % is treated as a variable placeholder.
Using MYSQL_PWD environment variable instead, which correctly
handles all special characters while still keeping the password
out of process args (ps aux).

Fixes: #issue-with-special-chars-in-mysql-passwords

commit.message:
Auto-discover YARP config files via directory watcher

Replace explicit YarpConfigPaths list with DirectoryJsonConfigurationProvider
that auto-discovers yarp-config-*.json files in YarpConfigDirectory (defaults
to /data/shared/). Uses FileSystemWatcher to detect new, changed, and deleted
files at runtime — adding a new operator no longer requires a proxy restart.

- DirectoryJsonConfigurationProvider: debounced file watcher, full rebuild on
  each change, last-known-good preservation on parse failure, temp file filtering
- DirectoryJsonConfigurationSource + AddJsonDirectory() extension
- 8 new tests: discovery, merge, ignore non-matching, missing directory,
  malformed file skip, runtime new file detection, deletion, temp file filtering
- Removed YarpConfigPaths from ProxyConfig and proxy.env.example
- Updated ADR-018 deployment example and consequences
